attractions.name: Brooks Falls
attractions.season: Platforms staffed Jun 1-Sep 17; July = salmon leaping, Sep = bears on spawned-out fish
attractions.cost: ~$800-1,000 day trip from King Salmon; ~$1,540 from Anchorage
attractions.blurb: Elevated platform over a 6ft falls where brown bears catch sockeye mid-air; the iconic Alaska bear photo.
attractions.tip: July platform time is rationed to 1hr when a queue forms; the 4pm-close hours have the shortest wait.
attractions.duration_note: full day from Anchorage or King Salmon

attractions.name: Brooks Camp
attractions.season: Lodge and full services Jun 1-Sep 17; campground May 1-Oct 31
attractions.cost: Campground $18/person/night (Jun-Sep 17); Brooks Lodge via annual lottery
attractions.blurb: The only developed base inside Katmai: lodge, campground, ranger bear school, and the trail to the falls.
attractions.tip: Lodge rooms go by Dec lottery; campground blocks drop Jan 7/Feb 7/Mar 7 on recreation.gov and sell out fast.
attractions.duration_note: 2-3 nights to actually relax into it

attractions.name: Valley of Ten Thousand Smokes
attractions.aka: Three Forks Overlook, Robert F. Griggs Visitor Center
attractions.season: Bus tour runs daily Jun-mid Sep from Brooks Camp
attractions.blurb: Ash desert buried 700ft deep by the 1912 Novarupta eruption; 23mi 4WD bus road ends at Three Forks Overlook.
attractions.tip: Bus leaves ~8am, back ~4pm - so it eats a whole bear day. Book it as a separate day, and pack wind layers.
attractions.duration_note: full day out of Brooks Camp

attractions.name: Novarupta
attractions.aka: Novarupta lava dome, Mount Katmai caldera
attractions.season: Snow-free window is short: mid-Jul to early Sep
attractions.blurb: Vent of the largest eruption of the 20th century (1912); a squat lava dome plugging the valley's source.
attractions.tip: No maintained trail: a 12mi+ off-trail slog with river fords from Three Forks. Most visitors just view it from the bus.

attractions.name: Hallo Bay
attractions.season: Sedge-flat grazing Jun; clamming Jul; salmon Aug-Sep
attractions.cost: ~$800-950 day trip from Homer or Kodiak
attractions.blurb: Wide sedge meadow on the Shelikof coast where bears graze and dig clams at close range with no platform.
attractions.tip: Ground-level viewing beats a platform for photos. Flights are tide- and fog-dependent; build in a spare day.

attractions.name: Geographic Harbor
attractions.season: Chum and pink salmon runs peak late Jul-Aug
attractions.blurb: Sheltered fjord-like inlet at the head of Amalik Bay; bears fish the shallow creek mouths from the beach.
attractions.tip: Late July-August only - before the run there are few bears. Multi-day live-aboard boats work this coast too.
attractions.duration_note: full-day flight from Homer or Kodiak

attractions.name: King Salmon Visitor Center
attractions.season: Visitor center open summer; airport year-round
attractions.blurb: Airport town that gates all Katmai travel; joint NPS/USFWS center handles bear orientation and flight staging.
attractions.tip: There is no road in - jets from Anchorage only. Weather delays are normal; never book a same-day connection.

attractions.name: Aniakchak Caldera
attractions.season: Weather window mid-Jun to Aug; fog and williwaws are constant
attractions.blurb: 6mi-wide collapsed caldera holding Surprise Lake, which drains through The Gates in a 1,500ft rift.
attractions.tip: The least-visited NPS unit in the US - often under 200 visitors a year. Budget spare days: charters often can't land.

attractions.name: McNeil River Falls
attractions.season: Permit season Jun 7-Aug 25 in 4-day blocks; Mikfik sockeye Jun, McNeil chum peaks Jul
attractions.blurb: The densest brown bear gathering on earth - up to 74 bears counted at once, capped at 10 permitted viewers a day.
attractions.tip: Lottery-only: apply by March 1 for the following summer. You camp 4 days; there is no lodge and no day access.

attractions.name: Naknek
attractions.season: Fishery explodes late Jun-mid Jul; dead quiet otherwise
attractions.blurb: Cannery town at the Naknek River mouth, epicentre of the world's largest sockeye run (~50M+ fish a season).
attractions.tip: 15mi paved road from King Salmon. Late June the beach is wall-to-wall setnets - the real working Alaska.
attractions.duration_note: half-day side trip from King Salmon

attractions.name: Silver Salmon Creek
attractions.season: Sedge grazing Jun; clam digging Jul; silver salmon mid-Aug-Sep
attractions.cost: ~$750-900 day trip from Homer or Anchorage
attractions.blurb: Salt marsh and beach on Cook Inlet where brown bear sows graze sedge and dig clams within 30-50 yards.
attractions.tip: Closest thing to guaranteed bears from Homer (~1hr flight). June sedge and Sep silvers both work; July is clams.

attractions.name: Chinitna Bay
attractions.season: Peak sedge grazing Jun-Jul; salmon at the head of the bay Aug
attractions.blurb: Tidal flats ~17mi SW of Silver Salmon Creek; 20+ brown bears at once on the salt marsh in a good June.
attractions.tip: Tide-dependent beach landings - operators pick Chinitna or Silver Salmon that morning based on tide and fog.

attractions.name: Richard Proenneke Cabin
attractions.aka: Upper Twin Lake, Alone in the Wilderness cabin
attractions.season: Floatplane access once ice is out, roughly mid-Jun to mid-Sep
attractions.cost: Free to enter; ~$600-900 floatplane charter from Port Alsworth
attractions.blurb: Hand-built 1968 log cabin of Dick Proenneke, kept as he left it; NPS volunteers open it for tours in summer.
attractions.tip: Charter from Port Alsworth or Anchorage. Combine with a Twin Lakes overnight - the cabin alone is a 30-min stop.
attractions.duration_note: half-day charter, or a night at Twin Lakes

attractions.name: Port Alsworth
attractions.season: Visitor center staffed summer; flights year-round weather permitting
attractions.blurb: Lake-shore village of ~180 that serves as Lake Clark's only visitor center and trailhead base.
attractions.tip: ~1hr flight from Anchorage via Lake Clark Pass. Only trailheads in the park start here - everywhere else is bush.

attractions.name: Tanalian Falls Trail
attractions.season: Snow-free roughly Jun-Sep
attractions.blurb: 2.4mi each way from Port Alsworth to a broad falls on the Tanalian River; the park's only maintained trail.
attractions.tip: Extend to Kontrashibuna Lake or the 4mi Tanalian Mountain climb for the classic overhead Lake Clark view.

attractions.name: Lake Clark Pass
attractions.season: Flown year-round; clearest air Jun-Sep
attractions.cost: Included in any Anchorage-Port Alsworth flight (~$300 one way)
attractions.blurb: Narrow glacier-walled pass through the Chigmits; the standard low-level bush route from Anchorage into the park.
attractions.tip: Free flightseeing if you're already flying to Port Alsworth - ask for a left window heading west.

attractions.name: Kodiak
attractions.season: Year-round; greenest and driest Jun-Aug, wettest Oct-Dec
attractions.cost: Free to visit; ~$250 flight or ~$100 ferry from Homer
attractions.blurb: Working fishing port of ~5,500 with Russian-era roots, ~100mi of road, and a Coast Guard base; gateway to bears.
attractions.tip: Alaska Airlines jets from Anchorage (~1hr) or the Tustumena ferry from Homer. It rains ~200 days a year - pack for it.
attractions.duration_note: 3-4 days to use the road system and fly out for bears

attractions.name: Fort Abercrombie State Historical Park
attractions.season: Open year-round; military history museum staffed summer only
attractions.blurb: WWII coastal battery, bunkers and 8-inch gun mounts on a spruce headland above the sea, 4mi from downtown.
attractions.tip: Best cliff-top whale and puffin watching on the road system. The bunker museum only opens a few days a week.

attractions.name: Holy Resurrection Russian Orthodox Cathedral
attractions.season: Year-round; interior viewing by service or arrangement
attractions.blurb: Blue-and-white 1945 cathedral of the oldest Orthodox parish in the Americas (founded 1794); relics of St Herman.
attractions.tip: Exterior any time; interior is usually open only around Saturday vespers and Sunday liturgy. Uphill from Mission Rd.

attractions.name: Alutiiq Museum
attractions.season: Open year-round Tue-Fri 10am-4pm, Sat noon-4pm
attractions.blurb: Alutiiq/Sugpiaq-run museum of 7,500 years of island culture - masks, kayak gear, and active archaeology.
attractions.tip: Small but the best Alutiiq collection anywhere; closed Sun-Mon. Pair with the Russian cathedral up the block.

attractions.name: Kodiak History Museum
attractions.season: Open Wed-Sat year-round; Fri open late to 7pm
attractions.blurb: Russian-American Company warehouse of 1808 - one of four surviving Russian buildings in the US - now a museum.
attractions.tip: Renamed from the Baranov Museum and reopened after renovation; closed Sun-Tue. 5-min walk from the ferry dock.

attractions.name: Kodiak National Wildlife Refuge Visitor Center
attractions.season: Open year-round, Tue-Sat; refuge itself is fly-in only
attractions.blurb: Free center at 402 Center Ave with a 36ft gray whale skeleton; the planning desk for 1.9M-acre Kodiak refuge.
attractions.tip: Start here if you want a refuge cabin - the 9 public-use cabins are $45/night by quarterly lottery.

attractions.name: Frazer Lake Fish Pass
attractions.season: Sockeye ascend the fish ladder late Jun through Aug
attractions.blurb: Fish ladder at Frazer Lake's outlet where Kodiak brown bears fish; 5-10 bears at once from grassy viewing knolls.
attractions.tip: 50-min floatplane from Kodiak then a 1.25mi walk. Cheapest reliable way to see the giant Kodiak subspecies.

attractions.name: Pillar Mountain
attractions.season: Gravel road usually drivable May-Oct; snow closes it in winter
attractions.blurb: 1,270ft hill directly behind town; steep gravel road to wind turbines and a full view over the harbor and islands.
attractions.tip: 2WD-doable when dry but rutted. Best at evening light; hosts the odd World Championship Mountain Man snow race.

attractions.name: Chiniak Highway and Pasagshak
attractions.aka: Kodiak road system, Pasagshak State Recreation Site
attractions.season: Open year-round; gravel sections rough after storms
attractions.blurb: 42mi of coast road to Chiniak plus the Pasagshak spur: beaches, bison ranch, WWII ruins, surf and silver salmon.
attractions.tip: Rent a car in Kodiak - it's the only way to use the island's road system, and vehicles sell out in summer.
attractions.duration_note: full day round trip from Kodiak

attractions.name: Kodiak Ferry Terminal
attractions.season: Year-round service to Homer; Aleutian chain sailings roughly monthly Apr-Oct
attractions.blurb: Downtown Alaska Marine Highway dock; the MV Tustumena's Homer-Kodiak run is ~9-10hrs across Shelikof Strait.
attractions.tip: The crossing is genuinely rough - take meds. Vehicle space books out months ahead for summer sailings.

attractions.name: Unalaska
attractions.season: Year-round; least brutal weather Jun-Aug, still fog and wind
attractions.blurb: Treeless volcanic island town of ~4,300, 800mi west of Anchorage; WWII battleground and the top US fishing port.
attractions.tip: Flights are notoriously fog-cancelled - allow 2 buffer days. The Tustumena ferry is the slow, cheaper alternative.

attractions.name: Church of the Holy Ascension
attractions.season: Year-round; interior access limited
attractions.blurb: 1894-96 cruciform Orthodox church, a National Historic Landmark, holding ~700 icons and artifacts.
attractions.tip: Photogenic from the outside in any weather; ask at the Museum of the Aleutians about interior access times.

attractions.name: Museum of the Aleutians
attractions.season: Summer (May-Sep) Tue-Sun 11am-4pm; winter Thu-Sun
attractions.blurb: Unangax̂ archaeology, Russian-era artifacts and WWII material covering 9,000 years of Aleutian occupation.
attractions.tip: The single best place to understand the 1942 Unangax̂ internment - a story most Alaska tours skip entirely.

attractions.name: Fort Schwatka
attractions.season: Road passable roughly May-Oct; snow and wind close it otherwise
attractions.blurb: Highest coastal gun battery ever built in the US - concrete bunkers and observation posts ~900ft up Mt Ballyhoo.
attractions.tip: You need an Ounalashka Corporation land permit (buy in town) to walk any of this. High-clearance 4WD for the road.

attractions.name: Aleutian WWII National Historic Area Visitor Center
attractions.season: Staffed roughly Jun-Sep; off-season by appointment
attractions.blurb: NPS-affiliated center in the restored 1942 Naval Air Station aerology building by the airport; tells the forgotten war.
attractions.tip: Pick up the free Fort Schwatka self-guided booklet and the land permit info here before driving up Ballyhoo.

attractions.name: Dutch Harbor
attractions.season: Pollock A-season Jan-Apr; crab and salmon deliveries fall-winter
attractions.cost: Free to walk the harbor road
attractions.blurb: The #1 US fishing port by volume for decades; the Deadliest Catch crabbers and pollock factory trawlers tie up here.
attractions.tip: Walk the causeway and small-boat harbor for the boats. Bering Sea snow crab quotas collapsed - fewer crabbers now.

attractions.name: MV Tustumena Aleutian Chain Run
attractions.season: Chain sailings roughly monthly late Apr-early Oct; no winter chain service
attractions.cost: ~$500-700 pp Homer-Dutch Harbor one way, cabin extra
attractions.blurb: 3-4 day ferry from Homer/Kodiak out the Alaska Peninsula to Dutch Harbor, calling at Chignik, Sand Point, King Cove.
attractions.tip: Only a handful of chain sailings a season and they sell out by spring. The 1964 vessel misses trips - keep slack.
attractions.duration_note: 3-4 days each way; ~7 days round trip

attractions.name: Adak Island
attractions.season: Alaska Airlines jets twice weekly (Wed/Sat) year-round; weather-dependent
attractions.blurb: Half-abandoned Cold War navy town of ~170 on a treeless volcanic island 1,200mi from Anchorage; ghost-town urbex.
attractions.tip: Only two Alaska Airlines flights a week - a missed one costs 3-4 days. Rent a truck locally; no other transport.

attractions.name: Attu Island
attractions.season: Rare charter/expedition-ship visits, Jun-Aug only
attractions.blurb: Far-western Aleutian island at 173°E, past the antimeridian; site of the only WWII land battle on North American soil.
attractions.tip: Uninhabited since the Coast Guard LORAN station closed in 2010 - reachable only by rare charter or expedition ship.
attractions.duration_note: part of a multi-week Aleutian expedition

attractions.name: St. Paul Island
attractions.season: Fur seals ashore Jun-Oct; seabird cliffs Jun-Aug; Asian vagrants late May and Sep
attractions.cost: ~$1,200+ airfare plus King Eider Hotel; birding tours ~$4,000-6,000
attractions.blurb: Bering Sea island with vast northern fur seal rookeries, million-bird cliffs and Asian rarities; Unangax̂ village of ~410.
attractions.tip: Aleutian Airways flies Anchorage-St. Paul a few days a week in summer; King Eider Hotel is the only lodging.

attractions.name: Ridge Wall Seabird Cliffs
attractions.season: Birds on the cliffs mid-Jun through Aug; gone by mid-Sep
attractions.blurb: Basalt cliffs with wooden blinds over nesting least/crested/parakeet auklets, both puffin species and red-legged kittiwakes.
attractions.tip: Red-legged kittiwake breeds almost nowhere else on earth. Shoot morning - afternoon fog usually swallows the cliffs.

attractions.name: St. George Island
attractions.season: Seabird season Jun-Aug; air service thin and unreliable
attractions.blurb: Smaller Pribilof with the largest seabird colony in the northern hemisphere - ~2.5M birds on 1,000ft cliffs.
attractions.tip: Far harder to reach than St. Paul and lodging is minimal; only worth it for serious birders with schedule slack.

attractions.name: Wood-Tikchik State Park
attractions.season: Floatplane season roughly mid-Jun to mid-Sep
attractions.blurb: Largest state park in the US at 1.6M acres: two chains of interconnected glacial lakes, world-class rainbow and salmon.
attractions.tip: Access is floatplane from Dillingham. Lake-to-lake float trips down the Agulowak/Wood River are the classic itinerary.

attractions.name: Round Island
attractions.season: Permit access May 1-Aug 15; peak walrus numbers Jun-Jul
attractions.blurb: Craggy Bering Sea island where up to 14,000 male Pacific walrus haul out on the beaches; also puffins and sea lions.
attractions.tip: 5-day ADF&G permits, first-come. You camp - no facilities - and boat landings get weathered out for days at a time.

attractions.name: Dillingham
attractions.season: Year-round; fishing frenzy late Jun-Jul
attractions.blurb: Bristol Bay hub of ~2,200 and the staging point for Wood-Tikchik, Togiak refuge and Round Island charters.
attractions.tip: Rooms are impossible and rates triple during the late-June sockeye opener - book flights and beds months out.

attractions.name: Bethel
attractions.season: Year-round; Kuskokwim 300 sled race Jan, Cama-i Dance Festival late Mar
attractions.blurb: Largest western Alaska hub (~6,300) on the Kuskokwim; Yup'ik cultural centre and gateway to Yukon Delta refuge.
attractions.tip: Not a tourist town - go for the Cama-i Dance Festival in late March or the Kuskokwim 300 in January, or don't go.

attractions.name: Yukon Delta National Wildlife Refuge
attractions.season: Shorebird and waterfowl nesting late May-Jul; frozen and empty otherwise
attractions.blurb: 19M-acre tundra wetland, the second largest US refuge - nesting ground for ~2M ducks and 100M shorebirds.
attractions.tip: No trails, no cabins, no roads - this is charter-and-camp country. Refuge HQ in Bethel is the only visitor facility.

attractions.name: Iliamna
attractions.season: Rainbow trout Jun and Sep-Oct; sockeye Jul; lodges close by early Oct
attractions.blurb: Airstrip village on Alaska's largest lake (1,000 sq mi); base for the Kvichak trophy rainbow fishery and lodge fly-outs.
attractions.tip: Gateway to Katmai's back door and the Pebble Mine controversy country. Serious anglers only - little else to do here.

attractions.name: Izembek National Wildlife Refuge
attractions.season: Entire Pacific brant population stages here late Sep-early Nov
attractions.blurb: One of the world's largest eelgrass beds; ~98% of Pacific black brant and most emperor geese stage here each fall.
attractions.tip: Cold Bay has a huge WWII-era runway but almost no services - the fall staging spectacle is the only reason to come.
